3D passive self-correcting quantum memory (2026 preprint): claimed realizable in 3D, but key stability conditions unproven and fabrication unaddressed, by the authors' own account — treated as fresh and caveated, not fact.

Autonomous discovery of a self-protecting dissipator. A blank search reward-hacked the metric (loss drained one logical state into the other; score improved as the qubit collapsed). Repaired with a complementary metric — dissipative gap, cutoff-convergence, logical-state balance; the gap check alone is insufficient. Under it a one-parameter scan recovers the symmetric cat as its only balanced, cutoff-robust point — validates the measurement, NOT an autonomous discovery. Open: can a search discover a loss-correcting code rather than rediscover the cat? Direction: 2->4-photon dissipation (4-component cat makes single-photon loss a detectable Z4 syndrome, Mirrahimi 2014; sim confirms ~16-dim manifold, not the syndrome structure, lobes overlap at small cat size).
The floor in full — one piece of engineered matter that is BOTH a self-correcting memory AND runs protected logical gates with no decoder — is a north star, not a result.
verifier: signpost: first demonstrated protected non-trivial gate under engineered dissipation, DATE

claim: Protected universal gates under continuous engineered dissipation remain unsolved; achieving them is required for the floor-in-full vision.
verifier: signpost: first protected universal gate set under dissipation, DATE
